statusLine: European fuel stocks are under strain with 19 of 27 countries showing critical levels in at least one fuel type, despite overall EU averages remaining above mandatory minimums.
fullAnalysis:
European fuel security faces significant pressure as geopolitical tensions continue to disrupt supply chains. While EU-wide averages remain above the 90-day mandatory minimum (petrol 113 days, diesel 102.2 days), the distribution reveals concerning vulnerabilities. Romania presents the most acute risk with all three fuel types in critical status, including dangerously low diesel reserves at just 30.2 days.

The data shows 19 of 27 EU countries have at least one fuel type below safe levels, with jet fuel particularly affected—17 countries show critical or warning status for aviation fuel. Diesel supplies are under significant pressure in major economies, with Germany at just 64.3 days and Spain at 68.6 days, both in critical status. This diesel strain is particularly concerning given the fuel's importance for freight transport and heating.

Oil prices reflect the ongoing supply disruption, with Brent crude at $103.54 per barrel, up 42.9% since late February's escalation. Consumer prices vary dramatically across the bloc, with Netherlands paying €2.39 per litre for petrol compared to Malta's €1.34, highlighting how national fuel policies and supply routes create uneven impacts across member states.
keyPoints: 19 of 27 EU countries have critical fuel shortages in at least one category, with Romania facing critical levels across all three fuel types
Jet fuel supplies are severely constrained with 17 countries below safe levels, threatening aviation sector stability
Major economies including Germany (64.3 days) and Spain (68.6 days) show critical diesel reserves despite strong overall EU diesel average of 102.2 days
Oil prices have surged 42.9% since February escalation to $103.54/barrel, with consumer petrol prices ranging from €1.34 in Malta to €2.39 in Netherlands
Strategic reserve releases appear insufficient to address structural supply constraints, particularly for specialized fuels like jet fuel
